title: Rocket Lab wins NASA award for three Electron launches
summary: NASA selected Rocket Lab to launch two science missions using three Electron rockets in 2027. This award highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting NASA's scientific exploration efforts.

title: Weather Stream releases first light imagery from GEMS2 microwave radiometer
summary: Weather Stream, a commercial weather satellite operator based in Boulder, Colorado, has captured its first light imagery from the GEMS2-Amethyst satellite's microwave radiometer. The satellite was launched on March 30 via SpaceX's Transporter 16 rideshare mission.

title: Space Force’s new MUOS satellites to use Boeing’s 702MP spacecraft platform
summary: The U.S. Space Force will deploy new MUOS satellites using Boeing's 702MP spacecraft platform, which has previously supported commercial and military missions.

title: Botswana signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Botswana has signed the Artemis Accords, joining the international framework for responsible space exploration. This marks the country's commitment to collaborative and sustainable space activities.

title: Small satellite operators confront a bottleneck to space access
summary: Small satellite operators face challenges accessing space due to reliance on SpaceX's rideshare missions, which have become a bottleneck for launch opportunities.

title: Finding solutions to jamming and spoofing
summary: Iridium's Tim Last discusses solutions to GPS jamming and spoofing challenges in the direct-to-device and IoT sectors during a Space Minds podcast episode. The conversation also touches on competition within these markets.

title: House Appropriations Committee approves $55.5 billion for U.S. Space Force
summary: The U.S. House Appropriations Committee has approved a $55.5 billion budget for the U.S. Space Force. The committee also emphasized the need for increased competition in satellite communications and criticized funding methods for the Golden Dome.

title: Canceled NASA exploration projects suffered billions of dollars in overruns
summary: Four NASA exploration projects were canceled due to overruns that more than doubled their costs, with over $1 billion in additional increases expected. The projects had significant budget overruns before being terminated.

title: China schedules Long March 10B rocket launch and recovery attempt
summary: China plans to conduct the maiden flight of its Long March 10B rocket in July, aiming to recover the first stage at sea. The mission involves airspace and maritime safety notices for the launch.

title: Space Force seeks fresh bidders for satellite-control antennas
summary: The U.S. Space Force is seeking new bidders for satellite-control antennas after terminating a $1.7 billion contract. The program, known as SCAR, will be relaunched with fresh procurement.

title: Two 'super-puff' cotton candy exoplanets are the lightest gas giants ever discovered
summary: Astronomers discovered two 'super-puff' exoplanets with densities lower than cotton candy, orbiting the same star. These are the lightest gas giants ever observed.

title: This Prime Day, save nearly $700 on 'a camera of epic proportions'
summary: Canon EOS R5 mirrorless camera is on sale for nearly $700 off during Prime Day. The deal is time-sensitive as Prime Day is halfway over.

title: James Webb Space Telescope catches 6 galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed six galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe. A supermassive black hole is forming at the center of this galactic merger.

title: I got a sneak peek at space shuttle Endeavour's new home in California, and it's breathtaking (photos)
summary: The new Air & Space Center in California, showcasing space shuttle Endeavour, will open on November 13 after over 30 years of development. The facility is described as breathtaking in newly released photos.

title: Hosting a solar eclipse viewing party? Get money off solar eclipse multipacks this Prime Day
summary: Amazon Prime Day offers discounts on solar eclipse glasses multipacks from Galaxium, Helioclipse, and Rainbow Symphony for safe group viewing of the upcoming solar eclipse.

title: A SpaceX rocket will slam into the moon this August. Will we be able to see it?
summary: A spent SpaceX Falcon 9 upper stage is set to impact the moon in August. Experts are uncertain whether the crash will be visible from Earth.

title: 'F9' took a car into space, but 5 years on, just how ridiculous was the scene? We asked the experts
summary: Five years after SpaceX's Falcon 9 launched a Tesla Roadster into space, experts weigh in on the significance of the stunt. The mission, though initially seen as frivolous, had deeper scientific and technological implications.

title: Summer solstice seen from space | Space photo of the day for June 25, 2026
summary: A European weather satellite captured a striking image of the summer solstice from space. The photo was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 25, 2026.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. After 25 flights, it is now set to serve an educational mission.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket early Tuesday morning. This test marks a significant step in SpaceX's development of reusable reentry technologies.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space has transported the second stage of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket to the Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward its planned 2026 debut. The company continues advancing development of the fully reusable launch vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches are expected to take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace aborted the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is headquartered in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks prior.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for China's internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marked a significant milestone for Japan's space program after a previous failure.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. This milestone reflects ESA's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025. These events will outline planned activities, priorities, and opportunities for industry engagement.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance satellite servicing and maintenance. The technology could revolutionize in-space operations by providing versatile manipulation capabilities.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to enhance mission success.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission focused on addressing hacking threats in orbit and shared-compute modules on rideshare satellites. Space Fabric, SpaceComputer's solution, aims to enhance on-orbit security amid growing cyber risks.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal this month for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO. This initiative aims to enhance Japan's sovereign satellite communications capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Canada’s Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This collaboration aims to advocate for the interests of NGSO operators in the evolving space industry.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a new era for the reentry sector. The event highlights growing competition among startups in space reentry technology.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches, according to a report from the agency's inspector general.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Ubotica Raises $11M for Satellite-Based AI Tech
summary: Ubotica, a Dublin-based space AI company, raised $11 million to advance its AI-powered intelligence platform for maritime security. The funding will accelerate the commercialization of its satellite-based AI technology.

title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
summary: Hosted payloads are revolutionizing space access by reducing time, cost, and expertise requirements, allowing companies to deploy new capabilities more efficiently. This shift enables faster and more economical access to orbit compared to traditional self-built spacecraft models.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has unveiled a new antenna to expand its network capacity, enabling more data downlinks across a wider range of frequencies. This development supports the company's rapid global expansion of its ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: Japanese satellite reentry startup ElevationSpace raised $40 million in a Series B funding round, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million.

title: Infleqtion Teams With Voyager in New Quantum Space Initiative
summary: Infleqtion and Voyager Technologies are collaborating with other entities to advance U.S. quantum space initiatives. The partnership includes Monarch Quantum, Armada, and the University of Colorado Boulder.

title: Pegasus Launch Set to Deploy Servicer Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus rocket is scheduled to launch a servicing spacecraft to reboost NASA’s Swift Observatory before it re-enters Earth’s atmosphere. The mission aims to extend the observatory's operational life.

title: Mitsubishi Electric Contracts MDA Space for Digital Payload for Japanese Defense Satellite
summary: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation has contracted MDA Space to develop a digital payload for a next-generation Japanese defense communications satellite. The contract includes the construction of digital payloads and antennas.

title: Capella Validates Mynaric Optical Terminal on Latest SAR Satellite
summary: Capella Space validated a Mynaric optical communications terminal on its newest synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite, marking the first deployment of an optical terminal by Capella. The company also released the first SAR images from this satellite.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct the buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two satellites expected to become operational by 2029.

title: Space Force Awards Boeing $2 Billion MUOS Service Life Extension Contract
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Boeing a $2 billion contract to extend the service life of the Mobile User Objective System (MUOS) by building two additional satellites. The MUOS system, deployed in 2019, provides secure communications for military operations.

title: Amazon Leo to Connect Construction Equipment for Hitachi Construction Machinery
summary: Amazon Leo has partnered with Hitachi Construction Machinery to connect construction equipment as part of its enterprise service preview. This collaboration marks Amazon Leo's expansion into the construction industry.

title: The EU’s 2 GHz Plan: A Test Case for Sovereignty in Satcom
summary: The European Commission is proposing to re-authorize the 2 GHz Mobile Satellite Services band beyond 2027, highlighting the role of satellite connectivity in geopolitical considerations. This move is seen as a test case for EU sovereignty in satellite communications.

title: LEO Operators Launch New Industry Group for NGSO Issues
summary: A new trade association called SpaceConnect Association was launched by Amazon, Iridium, Telesat, and Globalstar to represent Non-Geostationary (NGSO) satellite issues. This group aims to address industry challenges and advocate for NGSO satellite operators.

title: Spanish IoT Startup FOSSA Secures $10M in Funding
summary: Spanish IoT startup FOSSA raised $10.5 million to expand its satellite capabilities globally. The funding will support international expansion and sovereign satellite deployment.
